markdown:
<definition term="Market">Any **arrangement** that **connects** **buyers** and **sellers**, enabling them to carry out an **exchange**.</definition>

1. **Individual demand **only focuses on the demand of one consumer.
2. **Market demand ** focuses on all the consumers in the market of a good or service.

### Individual demand


The **individual demand** is the various **quantities** of a **good** **or** **service** that an **individual consumer **is willing and able to buy at different possible prices during a particular time period, _ceteris paribus_.

![Figure 1: an individual's consumer demand for pizzas](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/fc5f0d70ab9ba8e3718cefad5443d420c6bf4723-1863x1445.png)

1. In Figure 1, it can be seen that when the price of a pizza is:
  1. $10, the individual is not willing or able to buy any units, hence the quantity demanded is 0.
  2. $8, the individual is willing and able to buy 2 pizzas.
  3. $6, the individual is willing and able to buy 4 pizzas.
2. By connecting all of these points we construct the i**ndividual** **demand** **curve** for this particular consumer.

### Market Demand


**Market demand** represents the **total** **quantity** that **all** **of** **the** **consumers in the market **together are willing and able to purchase at **various** **prices**, during a particular time period, _ceteris paribus_.

<callout type="note">The **market demand **is found by summing the all of the individual demands together.</callout>

![Figure 2: individual consumer demands form the market demand](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/0b60ae682dd394c03146bd8b969b90b3eb4b8563-2321x760.png)

1. In Figure 2, it can be seen how the **market demand** is obtained from the addition of **individual demands **(for a market of two consumers):
  1. There are two individual demand curves:
      1. Individual Consumer 1 ($D_1$)
    2. Individual Consumer 2 ($D_2$)
  2. When their **individual** **demands** are **added** **up**, we get the **market** **demand ($D_m$)**.
